panels.description: 1 if this admin is in degraded state (lost a peer, can't form quorum, etc.). Should be 0.
panels.title: Admin Degraded

panels.description: Nodes flagged as unhealthy or unreachable in the last health check sweep.
panels.title: Unhealthy Nodes

panels.description: Clusters with no owning admin. Should converge to 0 within a recompute cycle.
panels.title: Orphaned Clusters

panels.description: Clusters this admin currently owns.
panels.title: Assigned Clusters

panels.description: Currently-open gateway connections from this admin.
panels.title: Active Gateways

panels.description: Cumulative successful peer-admin connections.
panels.title: Peer Admins Connected

panels.description: Rate of proxy auth rejections. Sustained non-zero may indicate misconfigured clients or a brute force attempt.
panels.title: Proxy Auth Failures (rate)

panels.description: Sustained errors here mean events are stalling in Oban (broker connectivity / auth degraded).
panels.title: Broker Publish Errors (rate)

panels.description: Background scheduled jobs failing. Most should be 0; recurring exceptions indicate a bug or external dependency outage.
panels.title: Quantum Job Exceptions (rate)

panels.description: End-to-end execution flow: created → delivered → completed, plus background expiration and pruning sweeps.
panels.title: Execution Lifecycle (rate)

panels.description: Wall time from delivery to result reported by agent. Long tails point at slow commands or unresponsive nodes.
panels.title: Execution Duration (sent → result)

panels.description: Background batch delivery runs (Quantum). Each tick may deliver many executions.
panels.title: Delivery Batch Rate

panels.description: Number of executions delivered in the most recent batch run.
panels.title: Last Delivery Batch Size

panels.description: Rows deleted in the most recent pruning sweep. 0 when pruning is disabled or nothing was eligible.
panels.title: Last Pruning Run Deleted

panels.description: Time spent in the pruning batched-delete loop. Spikes can indicate a large backlog of finalised rows accumulating between runs.
panels.title: Pruning Duration (p95)

panels.description: Inbound proxy connection rate split by protocol and outcome.
panels.title: Connection Rate by Result

panels.description: local = handled here. remote = routed across Erlang dist to the cluster owner. Lots of remote means clients are hitting the wrong admin.
panels.title: Routing Mode Split

panels.description: Session duration percentiles. <1s spikes indicate failed connects; >5m is healthy persistent tunnels.
panels.title: Session Duration (p50/p95/p99)

panels.description: Forwarding time only (excludes handshake). Compare with session duration to spot handshake overhead.
panels.title: Tunnel Duration (p50/p95/p99)

panels.description: normal = clean EOF. deadline = total-duration cap fired (slowloris suspect). drain_timeout = grace window expired during shutdown.
panels.title: Tunnel Close Rate by Reason

panels.description: Bytes/sec by direction. up = client→target, down = target→client.
panels.title: Throughput (Bytes/sec)

panels.description: Which clusters drive proxy load.
panels.title: Connection Rate by Cluster

panels.description: Per-node HTTP health probe latency.
panels.title: Health Check Duration

panels.description: Per-node health check outcomes. failure rate sustained → unreachable nodes.
panels.title: Health Check Rate by Result

panels.description: Unhealthy/unreachable nodes in the most recent sweep.
panels.title: Unhealthy (Last Sweep)

panels.description: Per-cluster reconciliation runs (DB ↔ Netmaker). Errors retry via Oban.
panels.title: Cluster Reconciliation Rate

panels.description: Spikes mean Netmaker is slow or the cluster has many nodes.
panels.title: Cluster Reconciliation Duration

panels.description: Errors seen in the most recent reconciliation run.
panels.title: Recon Errors (Last Run)

panels.description: New Netmaker hosts created during last reconciliation.
panels.title: Nodes Added (Last Run)

panels.description: Hosts removed from Netmaker during last reconciliation.
panels.title: Nodes Removed (Last Run)

panels.description: Orphaned DB node records purged in last reconciliation.
panels.title: DB Records Deleted

panels.description: Periodic sweep that deletes long-absent admin entries from Netmaker.
panels.title: Zombie Admin Cleanup

panels.description: Per-step membership operations during admin-cluster join.
panels.title: Membership Step Rate

panels.description: Step latency. Big numbers usually mean Netmaker API is slow.
panels.title: Membership Step Duration (p95)

panels.description: End-to-end time from boot until this admin successfully joined its admin cluster.
panels.title: Full Membership Sequence Duration

panels.description: Completed membership sequences (mostly fires once at boot, then on rejoin).
panels.title: Membership Sequence Rate

panels.description: Peer discovery activity: scans, peer connect attempts, DNS resolutions.
panels.title: Discovery Rate

panels.description: Time to recompute cluster ownership and assignment state.
panels.title: Metadata Recomputation Duration

panels.description: Scheduled job runs by name and result.
panels.title: Quantum Job Execution Rate

panels.description: Per-job latency. Tune scheduler intervals if duration approaches the schedule period.
panels.title: Quantum Job Duration (p95)

panels.description: Sustained non-zero indicates a recurring job bug.
panels.title: Quantum Job Exceptions

panels.description: Gateway connect/disconnect events per cluster.
panels.title: Connection Events

panels.description: Currently-open gateway connections.
panels.title: Active Gateways

panels.description: Metrics scrape ops via gateway, by cluster, type, and result.
panels.title: Scrape Rate

panels.description: SSH credential verification attempts. Sustained failures may indicate brute force or a misconfigured client.
panels.title: Verification Rate

panels.description: Failed verifications inside the current view window.
panels.title: Failures in Window

panels.description: Self-update request completions by targeting strategy.
panels.title: Requests Completed Rate

panels.description: High failed counts mean agents unreachable or self_update_enabled=false.
panels.title: Triggered vs Failed (Last Request)

panels.description: Publish attempts by result. Sustained errors → broker connectivity / auth issues.
panels.title: Publish Rate by Result

panels.description: Broker publish latency. High p95/p99 may indicate back-pressure or remote-broker network latency.
panels.title: Publish Duration

panels.description: Sustained positive value = events piling up in Oban faster than the broker accepts them.
panels.title: Backlog Signal (enqueue − publish_ok)
